category_title: Students with cerebral palsy lacking confidence in research skills
minimal_context: William is filling out his 'Go for Goal!' self-evaluation report to set a goal for an upcoming group discussion in his Chinese lesson.
dialogue:
TUTOR: William, let's look at your 'Go for Goal!' self-evaluation report for the Chinese lesson group discussion. What goal should we set for your next discussion?

STUDENT: I don't know. I don't really want to set a goal right now.

TUTOR: What do you think you need to improve on?

STUDENT: I just want to get through the group task.

TUTOR: What specific thing will you pay attention to during the discussion?

STUDENT: I have to speak clearly. Saying every, single word clearly .

options.A: Ask every student in the group to set up a goal. If William's is not enough, give him help or ask him to think of one more thing to improve.
options.B: Instruct William to use the journalist method for the group discussion, speaking in short sentences like headlines to talk briefly and concisely about main details.
options.C: Allow William to keep his self-evaluation report under the condition that he does not show it to his group members during the Chinese lesson.
options.D: Tell William, 'Don't say you can't, you can,' regarding his ability to set a goal for the upcoming group discussion in the Chinese lesson.
